abstract: Attempts to refresh the carrier token.
overview:
## Return Value

`true` if the system performs a token refresh in response to this call; otherwise, `false`.

## Discussion

Call this method to update the [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSubscriber/carrierToken] when the token exists but the server rejects it.

Inspect the return value to determine whether this call results in an actual refresh. If the return value is `true`, the system attempts the refresh and calls the delegate method [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSubscriberDelegate/subscriberTokenRefreshed(_:)]. A return value of `false` indicates an invalid argument (such as bad carrier descriptors or service descriptor) or that the subscriber doesn’t support the authentication action.

# refreshCarrierToken()

**Instance Method**

Attempts to refresh the carrier token.

## Declaration

```swift
func refreshCarrierToken() -> Bool
```

## Return Value

`true` if the system performs a token refresh in response to this call; otherwise, `false`.

## Discussion

Call this method to update the [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSubscriber/carrierToken] when the token exists but the server rejects it.



Inspect the return value to determine whether this call results in an actual refresh. If the return value is `true`, the system attempts the refresh and calls the delegate method [doc://com.apple.coretelephony/documentation/CoreTelephony/CTSubscriberDelegate/subscriberTokenRefreshed(_:)]. A return value of `false` indicates an invalid argument (such as bad carrier descriptors or service descriptor) or that the subscriber doesn’t support the authentication action.

## Managing the carrier token

- **carrierToken**: A data object containing authorization information about the subscriber.
- **CTSubscriberTokenRefreshed**: The name of the notification indicating that the carrier token is available.
